<h1>Source code for booktx.errors</h1><div class="highlight"><pre>
<span></span><span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;User-facing error type for booktx.</span>

<span class="sd">``BooktxError`` carries a stable ``code`` attribute so callers (CLI, validation,</span>
<span class="sd">workflows) can branch on the error kind without parsing prose. It is defined</span>
<span class="sd">here rather than in :mod:`booktx.config` so that low-level helpers such as</span>
<span class="sd">:mod:`booktx.path_ids` can raise it without importing ``config`` (which would</span>
<span class="sd">create an import cycle).</span>

<span class="sd">:mod:`booktx.config` re-exports ``BooktxError`` and ``_err`` for backwards</span>
<span class="sd">compatibility; existing ``from booktx.config import BooktxError`` imports keep</span>
<span class="sd">working.</span>
<span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>

<span class="kn">from</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nn">__future__</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="kn">import</span> <span class="n">annotations</span>


<div class="viewcode-block" id="BooktxError">
<a class="viewcode-back" href="../../../api/#booktx.config.BooktxError">[docs]</a>
<span class="k">class</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nc">BooktxError</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="ne">Exception</span><span class="p">):</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;User-facing error from booktx. Carries a stable ``code`` attribute.&quot;&quot;&quot;</span>

<div class="viewcode-block" id="BooktxError.__init__">
<a class="viewcode-back" href="../../../api/#booktx.config.BooktxError.__init__">[docs]</a>
    <span class="k">def</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="fm">__init__</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="bp">self</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">code</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="n">message</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">-&gt;</span> <span class="kc">None</span><span class="p">:</span>
        <span class="nb">super</span><span class="p">()</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="fm">__init__</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">message</span><span class="p">)</span>
        <span class="bp">self</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">code</span> <span class="o">=</span> <span class="n">code</span></div>
</div>



<span class="k">def</span><span class="w"> </span><span class="nf">_err</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="o">*</span><span class="n">message_parts</span><span class="p">:</span> <span class="nb">str</span><span class="p">)</span> <span class="o">-&gt;</span> <span class="n">BooktxError</span><span class="p">:</span>
<span class="w">    </span><span class="sd">&quot;&quot;&quot;Construct a :class:`BooktxError` (helper kept for concise call sites).</span>

<span class="sd">    Message fragments are concatenated in order, so a long message may be</span>
<span class="sd">    split across several adjacent string literals at the call site.</span>
<span class="sd">    &quot;&quot;&quot;</span>
    <span class="k">return</span> <span class="n">BooktxError</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">code</span><span class="p">,</span> <span class="s2">&quot;&quot;</span><span class="o">.</span><span class="n">join</span><span class="p">(</span><span class="n">message_parts</span><span class="p">))</span>
</pre></div>
